title: Harvard Reframes Cancer Genetics Through an Aging Lens
description: Harvard Medical School published a major piece reframing how age-driven mutational landscapes shape cancer biology, arguing that the same genetic context that produces tumor risk also drives broader aging phenotypes. The work strengthens the case for treating cancer and biological aging as overlapping rather than separable problems.

title: FDA Clears Life Biosciences Yamanaka-Factor Trial in Glaucoma
description: As 2026 opens, the FDA has cleared Life Biosciences to run a Yamanaka-factor partial-reprogramming trial for a specific form of glaucoma, marking the first regulator-blessed test of cellular epigenetic reset in humans. The clearance gives the broader rejuvenation field a credible safety/efficacy yardstick that competitors must now hit.

title: Tariff Backlash Hits Biotech ETFs as ARKG Drops 2.6%
description: Genomic-revolution ETFs rolled over hard on Tuesday with ARKG down 2.62% even as broader biotech (XBI, IBB) inched higher, underscoring how single-name pressure on innovation pure-plays can decouple from the cap-weighted indices. Healthcare and medical devices held up better, with XLV +0.39% and HTEC +0.74%.

title: Multivitamin COSMOS Result Slows Two Mortality-Linked Epigenetic Clocks
description: A pre-specified COSMOS substudy in Nature Medicine (Li et al., March 9 2026) shows two years of daily multivitamin-multimineral use slowed PCGrimAge and PCPhenoAge — the two epigenetic clocks most predictive of mortality — by roughly four months of biological age. Effects were largest in adults whose biological age outpaced their chronological age at baseline.

title: Wedbush, Retro Bio, Function Health Anchor a Five-Year Capital Wave
description: Three independent data points — Wedbush sizing the longevity trade at $120B, Retro Bio chasing a $5B valuation backed by Sam Altman, and Function Health closing a $298M Series B — frame longevity as a fully institutional category in 2026 rather than a buzzword. The 12-month tracker still shows funding heavily skewed to outliers, with five $50M+ rounds capturing 81% of disclosed capital.

title: A New Way To Think About Age and Cancer Genetics
description: HMS researchers reframe how age-driven mutational landscapes shape cancer biology, strengthening the case that cancer and biological aging are overlapping rather than separable problems.

title: Cellular Rejuvenation Has the Potential to Reverse Aging
description: NYT Magazine deep dive on Altos Labs covers rat kidney rejuvenation, planned pig studies, and the first FDA-cleared human trial of partial reprogramming — dosed in eye tissue for glaucoma.

title: FightAging — STING Nitrosylation Drives Alzheimer Neuroinflammation
description: Scripps team shows S-nitrosylation of STING at cysteine 148 (SNO-STING) drives chronic neuroinflammation; blocking the modification protects synapses in Alzheimer mouse models, opening a new druggable node distinct from amyloid and tau.

title: FightAging — Young-Mouse FMT Reduces Liver Cancer in Aged Mice
description: Fecal microbiota transplant from young mice suppresses MDM2 and inflammation in old mice; zero of eight FMT-treated animals developed liver tumors versus two of eight controls, adding a preclinical data point to microbiome-driven rejuvenation.

title: Naked Mole-Rat Microbiome Stable for 30+ Years
description: Multi-decade comparison shows naked mole-rats maintain a stable microbiome across decades — unlike mice — with higher diversity in pregnant queens, reinforcing microbiome resilience as a feature of exceptional-longevity species.

title: Seragon SRN-901 Combo Extends Median Mouse Lifespan 33%
description: Combination of urolithin A, quercetin, NR, ALA and SRN-820 increased median mouse lifespan by 33% and reduced frailty, providing a translational template for multi-target geroprotector stacks now common in protocol papers.

title: Inflammation's Statin Moment Edges Closer
description: Editorial sweep of NLRP3 inhibitors, IL-33 antibodies, and IL-6 antagonists frames inflammaging as on the cusp of a statin-style mass-market repositioning, citing BioAge BGE-102 and AstraZeneca tozorakimab as bellwether assets.

title: New Senolytic Strategy Targets a Key Vulnerability in Ageing Cells
description: Imperial researchers identified GPX4-targeting chloroacetamides that selectively kill senescent cells via ferroptosis. Combination with cancer therapy cleared persistent senescent tumor cells in melanoma, prostate, and ovarian models (Nature Cell Biology).

title: Mesoblast Advances CAR-MSC Platform; 300-Patient Phase 3 Enrolled
description: Mesoblast (ASX:MSB) finished enrolling its 300-patient Phase 3 of rexlemestrocel-L in chronic low back pain and licensed Mayo Clinic CAR technology to upgrade MSC homing for IBD and CD19 lupus nephritis programs.

title: INBRAIN Completes First-in-Human Graphene BCI Enrollment
description: INBRAIN Neuroelectronics finished recruiting its graphene cortical interface study, with 8 of 10 patients implanted and no device-related adverse events. Final results expected later in 2026.

title: Sinclair: Human Epigenetic Reprogramming Trial Days Away
description: At Abundance360, David Sinclair said the first human partial-reprogramming trial using three Yamanaka factors will dose a patient's eye to test reversal of vision loss within weeks, framing 2026 as the year age reversal moves from speculation to early clinical signal.

title: Peter Attia Supplements List 2026
description: Updated public list of Dr. Peter Attia's supplements, sleep aids, and prescription medications including exact brands, dosages, and stated rationale.

title: Longevity Biotech Hits $3.74B in Q1 2026 Financing
description: Longevity.Technology's DLT tracker recorded $3.74B raised across 49 financings in Q1 2026, up 56% year-over-year, concentrated in epigenetic reprogramming and mitochondrial health.

title: Wedbush — Longevity Trade is a $120B Market
description: Wedbush analyst note frames longevity as a $120B addressable market following FDA clearance of the first cellular rejuvenation trial and Longeveron's positive Phase 2b laromestrocel data in age-related frailty.

title: Energy Span Reframes Fatigue as Early Aging Warning
description: New 'energy span' biomarker frames fatigue as an early window into mitochondrial decline, supplementing healthspan and lifespan as patient-facing metrics.

title: Targeting Longevity 2026 Berlin — Strategies to Target Aging
description: Recap of the 2nd World Congress on Targeting Longevity highlights partial reprogramming, organ-specific proteomic clocks, and AI-designed proteins as 2026's most promising vectors.

title: Rapamycin and Exercise: New Trial Finds Performance Decline
description: RAPA-EX-01 in sedentary older adults (6 mg/week sirolimus) found smaller red cells, more platelets, higher alkaline phosphatase, higher HbA1c, and higher LDL versus placebo — clinically modest but a useful counterweight to rapamycin enthusiasm.

title: Insilico Forms Industry-First Longevity Board
description: HKEX-listed Insilico Medicine (3696) launched the industry's first Longevity Board to oversee aging-research strategy across life models, biomarkers, and target identification.

title: BioAge BGE-102 Phase 1 Hits Best-in-Class hsCRP Reductions
description: Brain-penetrant oral NLRP3 inhibitor showed potential best-in-class hsCRP reductions in obese participants with no serious adverse events; Phase 2 cardiovascular-risk POC due H2 2026.

title: AstraZeneca Tozorakimab Wins Third Phase 3 COPD Trial
description: MIRANDA showed statistically significant reductions in moderate-to-severe COPD exacerbations on top of standard inhaled care, completing a clean three-trial Phase 3 sweep for the IL-33 antibody.

title: Validated Mechanisms, Strong Data Beat Hype in Longevity Investing
description: BioSpace interview with LongeVC and other longevity investors argues capital is shifting from broad anti-aging hype to mechanism-specific bets on age-related disease assets that pencil out as standard biotech investments.

title: Multivitamins Modestly Slow Epigenetic Aging in COSMOS RCT
description: In a 958-participant ancillary study (Li et al., Nature Medicine 2026), two years of multivitamin-multimineral use slowed all five tested epigenetic clocks, with statistically significant slowing in PCGrimAge and PCPhenoAge — equivalent to roughly four months of biological aging.

title: Klotho Neurosciences Initiates Klotho Clock Diagnostic
description: Klotho Neurosciences (NASDAQ: KLTO) is developing two genomics-based diagnostics — a quantitative DNA methylation assay of the alpha-Klotho gene and a 9-gene Klotho Clock — to be used in clinical trials and offered as a longevity service.

title: Retro Bio Chases $5B Valuation in Longevity Mega-Round
description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman-backed Retro Bio is closing in on a record longevity fundraise at a $5B valuation despite no clinical data yet, deploying epigenetic editing and cell-replacement therapies to add 10 years to human lifespan.

title: Longevity Biotech Investment 2026 Set for Breakout Year
description: DLT Chatbot projections converge on $8-9B for full-year 2026 longevity biotech investment, with $10B-plus possible if macro and clinical catalysts cooperate.
